sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_decimals (id UInt32, dec_a Decimal64(5), dec_b Decimal64(5));
INSERT INTO test_decimals VALUES (1, toDecimal64(10.12345, 5), toDecimal64(2.5, 5)), (2, toDecimal64(NULL, 5), toDecimal64(0.0, 5)), (3, toDecimal64(-7.5, 5), toDecimal64(3.25, 5));
SELECT id, divideDecimal(dec_a, dec_b, 3) AS result_scale_3 FROM test_decimals WHERE EXISTS (SELECT * FROM test_decimals WHERE dec_b > 2.0) ORDER BY id;

SELECT id, divideDecimal(dec_a, divideDecimal(dec_b, 0.0, 2), 2) AS nested_division_result FROM test_decimals WHERE EXISTS (SELECT * FROM numbers(5) WHERE number < 2) GROUP BY id HAVING result_scale_3 IS NOT NULL;
CREATE TABLE test_numbers AS SELECT number FROM numbers(20);
SELECT EXISTS(SELECT * FROM test_numbers WHERE number > 15) AS exists_gt_15, EXISTS(SELECT * FROM test_numbers WHERE number < 5) AS exists_lt_5;
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_decimals WHERE EXISTS (SELECT id FROM test_decimals WHERE divideDecimal(dec_a, dec_b, 4) IS NOT NULL) AND dec_a IS NOT NULL;
SELECT id, divideDecimal(MAX(dec_a), MIN(dec_b), 3) AS max_div_min FROM test_decimals GROUP BY id HAVING EXISTS (SELECT * FROM test_decimals WHERE dec_b IS NOT NULL);
INSERT INTO test_decimals VALUES (4, toDecimal64(0.00001, 5), toDecimal64(1.00001, 5));
SELECT id, divideDecimal(dec_a, dec_b, 6) FROM test_decimals WHERE EXISTS (SELECT * FROM test_decimals WHERE dec_a > 0) AND divideDecimal(dec_a, dec_b, 6) < 0 LIMIT 1;
